---
title: "BVS.DAdj: Bayesian Variable Selection with Dual-Adjacency Network-Informed Ising Priors in R"
author:
  - name: Yubing Yao
    affiliation: University of Massachusetts Amherst
    email: yyao@umass.edu
  - name: Mahlet Tadesse
    affiliation: Georgetown University
    email: mgt26@georgetown.edu
  - name: Raji Balasubramanian
    affiliation: University of Massachusetts Amherst School of Public Health
    email: rbalasub@schoolph.umass.edu
abstract: >
  This article introduces the **BVS.DAdj** R package, a comprehensive computational 
  toolkit for Bayesian variable selection in logistic regression models. The package 
  uniquely implements Markov random field (MRF) priors that incorporate dual-adjacency 
  structures among predictors, allowing for the integration of multiple sources of 
  structural information. **BVS.DAdj** provides two robust Markov chain Monte Carlo 
  (MCMC) sampling strategies---Metropolis-Hastings (MH) and Pólya-Gamma (PG) data 
  augmentation. Each strategy supports five adjacency configurations: fixed, dual-fixed, 
  graphical lasso, Bayesian Gaussian graphical models (GGM), and combined GGM-plus-fixed. 
  To ensure computational efficiency, both dense ($p < n$) and sparse high-dimensional 
  ($p \gg n$) backends are implemented in C++ utilizing **Rcpp** and **RcppArmadillo**. 
  In this paper, we detail the underlying statistical framework, outline the package API, 
  and demonstrate its practical application through extensive simulation studies and 
  real-data analyses under both moderate ($n = 500, p = 100$) and high-dimensional 
  ($n = 200, p = 1000$) settings.
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
bibliography: references.bib
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    fig_caption: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: show
    df_print: kable
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{BVS.DAdj: R Journal Paper}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
is_truthy <- function(x) {
  tolower(trimws(as.character(x))) %in% c("1", "true", "t", "yes", "y", "on")
}

quick_env <- Sys.getenv("BVS_DADJ_QUICK_CHECK", unset = "")
quick_opt <- getOption("bvs.dadj.quick_check", FALSE)
quick_check <- is_truthy(quick_env) || isTRUE(quick_opt)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5,
  cache = TRUE,
  cache.extra = if (quick_check) "quick-check-fixed-pip05-eta-grid-v1" else "full-run-n5000-b2500-fixed-pip05-eta-grid-v1"
)

# Helper for quick smoke-test runs:
# set Sys.setenv(BVS_DADJ_QUICK_CHECK = "true") before render.
mcmc_scale <- function(full_niter, full_burnin, full_thin = 1L,
                       quick_niter = 500L, quick_burnin = 200L, quick_thin = 1L) {
  if (isTRUE(quick_check)) {
    list(
      niter = as.integer(quick_niter),
      burnin = as.integer(quick_burnin),
      thin = as.integer(quick_thin)
    )
  } else {
    list(
      niter = as.integer(full_niter),
      burnin = as.integer(full_burnin),
      thin = as.integer(full_thin)
    )
  }
}

cat(sprintf("BVS_DAdj_paper quick-check mode: %s\n", if (quick_check) "ON" else "OFF"))

# Robust ACF plotting helper:
# Handles constant or non-finite chains to avoid plot failures during vignette render.
safe_acf_plot <- function(x, lag.max = 50L, main = "", col = "steelblue") {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]

  if (length(x) < 2L) {
    plot.new()
    title(main = main)
    mtext("Insufficient finite samples for ACF", side = 3, line = -1.5, cex = 0.85)
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  v <- stats::var(x)
  if (!is.finite(v) || v <= .Machine$double.eps) {
    lags <- 0:as.integer(lag.max)
    acf_vals <- c(1, rep(0, length(lags) - 1L))
    plot(lags, acf_vals,
      type = "h", lwd = 2, col = col,
      ylim = c(-0.05, 1.05), xlab = "Lag", ylab = "ACF", main = main
    )
    points(lags, acf_vals, pch = 16, cex = 0.5, col = col)
    mtext("Constant chain: ACF undefined", side = 3, line = 0.1, cex = 0.75)
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  stats::acf(x, lag.max = as.integer(lag.max), main = main, col = col)
}

safe_gelman_df <- function(fits, vars = NULL, autoburnin = FALSE) {
  gd <- tryCatch(
    bvs_gelman_diag(fits, vars = vars, autoburnin = autoburnin),
    error = function(e) e
  )

  if (inherits(gd, "error")) {
    return(data.frame(
      Parameter = "NA",
      Point_est = NA_real_,
      Upper_CI = NA_real_,
      Note = conditionMessage(gd),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }

  psrf <- as.data.frame(gd$psrf, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (ncol(psrf) < 2L) {
    return(data.frame(
      Parameter = "NA",
      Point_est = NA_real_,
      Upper_CI = NA_real_,
      Note = "Gelman output did not contain PSRF columns.",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  names(psrf)[1:2] <- c("Point_est", "Upper_CI")
  psrf$Point_est <- as.numeric(psrf$Point_est)
  psrf$Upper_CI <- as.numeric(psrf$Upper_CI)

  param <- rownames(psrf)
  if (is.null(param) || length(param) != nrow(psrf)) {
    param <- rep("", nrow(psrf))
  }
  param <- trimws(as.character(param))
  blank <- which(!nzchar(param))
  if (length(blank) > 0L) {
    if (is.null(vars)) {
      beta_labels <- paste0("Beta", seq_len(length(blank)))
    } else {
      vars <- as.integer(vars)
      beta_labels <- paste0("Beta", vars[seq_len(min(length(vars), length(blank)))])
      if (length(beta_labels) < length(blank)) {
        beta_labels <- c(
          beta_labels,
          paste0("Beta", seq_len(length(blank) - length(beta_labels)) + length(beta_labels))
        )
      }
    }
    param[blank] <- beta_labels
  }

  out <- data.frame(
    Parameter = param,
    Point_est = psrf$Point_est,
    Upper_CI = psrf$Upper_CI,
    Note = "",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  out
}

gelman_method_summary <- function(gelman_df, method_name, rhat_threshold = 1.1) {
  keep <- is.finite(gelman_df$Point_est)
  if (!any(keep)) {
    return(data.frame(
      Method = method_name,
      N_parameters = 0L,
      Max_Rhat = NA_real_,
      Median_Rhat = NA_real_,
      N_Rhat_gt_1_1 = NA_integer_,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  data.frame(
    Method = method_name,
    N_parameters = sum(keep),
    Max_Rhat = round(max(gelman_df$Point_est[keep], na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    Median_Rhat = round(stats::median(gelman_df$Point_est[keep], na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    N_Rhat_gt_1_1 = sum(gelman_df$Point_est[keep] > rhat_threshold, na.rm = TRUE),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

extract_beta_chain <- function(fit, j) {
  b <- fit$beta
  if (is.matrix(b)) {
    j <- max(1L, min(as.integer(j), ncol(b)))
    return(as.numeric(b[, j]))
  }
  if (is.list(b)) {
    n_save <- length(b)
    out <- numeric(n_save)
    for (s in seq_len(n_save)) {
      bs <- b[[s]]
      if (is.null(bs) || length(bs) == 0) {
        out[s] <- 0
      } else if (is.matrix(bs) && ncol(bs) >= 2) {
        ids <- as.integer(bs[, 1])
        vals <- as.numeric(bs[, 2])
        hit <- which(ids == as.integer(j))
        out[s] <- if (length(hit) > 0) vals[hit[1]] else 0
      } else if (is.numeric(bs) && length(bs) >= as.integer(j)) {
        out[s] <- as.numeric(bs[as.integer(j)])
      } else {
        out[s] <- 0
      }
    }
    return(out)
  }
  return(numeric(0))
}

plot_two_chain_trace_panels <- function(fit1, fit2, beta_idx, method_label) {
  beta_idx <- as.integer(beta_idx)
  for (j in beta_idx) {
    ch1 <- extract_beta_chain(fit1, j)
    ch2 <- extract_beta_chain(fit2, j)
    y_lim <- range(c(ch1, ch2), finite = TRUE)
    if (length(y_lim) != 2 || !all(is.finite(y_lim))) y_lim <- c(-1, 1)
    plot(ch1,
      type = "l", col = "steelblue", lwd = 1,
      main = paste0(method_label, ": beta_", j),
      xlab = "Saved iteration", ylab = "beta",
      ylim = y_lim
    )
    lines(ch2, col = "firebrick", lwd = 1)
    legend("topright",
      legend = c("Chain 1", "Chain 2"),
      col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
      lty = 1, cex = 0.7, bty = "n"
    )
  }
}

# Fixed decision threshold used across both simulation and real-data examples.
# Eta/Moller tuning is done under this fixed threshold.
pip_threshold_fixed <- 0.5
sim_target_fdr <- 0.05

# Default fallbacks; overwritten by eta-grid tuning chunks in simulation sections.
default_hpars_sim1 <- list(
  mu = qlogis(0.02),
  eta1_sd = 0.10,
  eta2_sd = 0.06,
  e_eta = 1.0,
  f_eta = 25.0,
  mu_tilde = qlogis(0.01),
  eta1_tilde = 0.015,
  eta2_tilde = 0.010
)

default_hpars_sim2 <- list(
  mu = qlogis(0.02),
  eta1_sd = 0.06,
  eta2_sd = 0.05,
  e_eta = 1.0,
  f_eta = 30.0,
  mu_tilde = qlogis(0.005),
  eta1_tilde = 0.012,
  eta2_tilde = 0.008
)

hpars_sim1 <- default_hpars_sim1
hpars_sim2 <- default_hpars_sim2
hpars_real <- default_hpars_sim1
hpars_realhd <- default_hpars_sim2

coalesce_hpars_from_tune <- function(tune_obj, fallback, mu_override = NULL) {
  out <- fallback
  best <- tryCatch(tune_obj$best, error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.data.frame(best) && nrow(best) >= 1L) {
    b <- best[1, , drop = FALSE]
    for (nm in c("eta1_sd", "eta2_sd", "e_eta", "f_eta", "mu_tilde", "eta1_tilde", "eta2_tilde")) {
      if (nm %in% names(b)) {
        v <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(b[[nm]][1]))
        if (is.finite(v)) out[[nm]] <- v
      }
    }
  }
  if (!is.null(mu_override)) {
    muv <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(mu_override)[1])
    if (is.finite(muv)) out$mu <- muv
  }
  out
}

hpars_tag <- function(h) {
  raw <- sprintf(
    "mu%.3f_e1%.3f_e2%.3f_e%.1f_f%.1f_mt%.3f_t1%.3f_t2%.3f",
    as.numeric(h$mu), as.numeric(h$eta1_sd), as.numeric(h$eta2_sd),
    as.numeric(h$e_eta), as.numeric(h$f_eta), as.numeric(h$mu_tilde),
    as.numeric(h$eta1_tilde), as.numeric(h$eta2_tilde)
  )
  tag <- gsub("-", "m", raw, fixed = TRUE)
  tag <- gsub("\\.", "p", tag)
  tag
}
```

# Introduction

In metabolic case-control studies and high-throughput biological experiments, a primary analytical objective is identifying which subset of numerous candidate variables (e.g., metabolites, genes) is genuinely associated with a specific disease outcome. These variables frequently exhibit intricate dependency structures arising from multiple sources. For instance, biological pathway databases provide foundational, albeit partial, knowledge of inter-variable relationships, while data-driven network estimation methods can reveal latent conditional dependencies.

Standard penalization techniques, such as the LASSO [@Tib1996] and its subsequent extensions [@ZH2005; @YL2006; @Zou2006], perform variable selection by applying shrinkage to regression coefficients. However, these methods often fail to identify weakly associated variables that are correlated with stronger signals. Bayesian variable selection utilizing spike-and-slab priors [@GM1993; @GM1997] offers a principled alternative, seamlessly quantifying selection uncertainty through posterior inclusion probabilities (PIPs).

Building upon the Markov random field (MRF) framework introduced by @LZ2010 and @STV2011, the **BVS.DAdj** package extends the traditional Ising prior on selection indicators $\boldsymbol{\gamma}$ to incorporate *two independent* adjacency structures simultaneously---one derived from prior biological knowledge and another estimated empirically from the data. This dual-adjacency formulation facilitates the borrowing of strength across diverse sources of structural information, enhancing the robustness of the variable selection process.

To address the computational challenges inherent in fitting complex Bayesian models, the package is engineered with 12 highly optimized C++ backends. These are accessed seamlessly through two user-facing R functions, `bvs_mh()` and `bvs_pg()`, providing a comprehensive suite of tools spanning various sampler types, adjacency sources, and dimensionalities.

The remainder of this article is organized as follows. Section 2 delineates the statistical model and prior specifications. Section 3 outlines the MCMC sampling strategies employed. Section 4 details the package architecture and API. Sections 5 and 6 present extensive simulation studies demonstrating performance in moderate-dimensional and high-dimensional settings, respectively. Section 7 presents real-data applications, and Section 8 concludes with a summary of the software's contributions.

# Model Formulation and Priors {#sec-model}

## Logistic Regression Model

Consider a case-control study with a binary outcome vector $\mathbf{y} = (y_1, \ldots, y_n)^\top$ where $y_k \in \{0, 1\}$, and a design matrix $\mathbf{X} = (X_{ik})_{n \times p}$ comprising $p$ candidate predictors for $n$ observations. The relationship is modeled via logistic regression:
$$
\text{logit}\,\Pr(y_k = 1) = \alpha + \mathbf{X}_k \boldsymbol{\beta}, \quad k = 1, \ldots, n
$$
where $\alpha$ is the intercept and $\boldsymbol{\beta} = (\beta_1, \ldots, \beta_p)^\top$ represents the vector of regression coefficients.

## Spike-and-Slab Prior for Variable Selection

To facilitate variable selection, we introduce binary selection indicators $\boldsymbol{\gamma} = (\gamma_1, \ldots, \gamma_p)^\top \in \{0,1\}^p$. A point-mass mixture prior is placed on each coefficient:
$$
\beta_j \mid \gamma_j, \sigma^2 \sim \gamma_j \cdot \mathcal{N}(\beta_0, \sigma^2) + (1 - \gamma_j) \cdot \delta_0(\beta_j), \quad j = 1, \ldots, p
$$
The intercept is assigned a conjugate normal prior $\alpha \sim \mathcal{N}(\alpha_0, h_\alpha \sigma^2)$ with $h_\alpha$ set to a large value to remain non-informative. The variance component follows an inverse-gamma distribution:
$$
\sigma^2 \sim \text{Inverse-Gamma}\!\left(\frac{\nu_0}{2},\; \frac{\nu_0 \sigma_0^2}{2}\right)
$$

## Variant MRF Prior with Dual Adjacency

Let $\mathbf{R}$ and $\mathbf{R}'$ denote two $p \times p$ symmetric binary adjacency matrices, encoding two independent sources of structural information among the predictors. The prior on the selection indicators $\boldsymbol{\gamma}$ is formulated as an Ising/MRF model:
$$
p(\boldsymbol{\gamma} \mid \mu, \eta_1, \eta_2) = \frac{1}{Z(\mu,\eta_1,\eta_2)}\exp\!\Big(\mu\,\mathbf{1}_p^\top \boldsymbol{\gamma} + \eta_1\, \boldsymbol{\gamma}^\top \mathbf{R}\, \boldsymbol{\gamma} + \eta_2\, \boldsymbol{\gamma}^\top \mathbf{R}'\, \boldsymbol{\gamma}\Big)
$$
where:

* $\mu$ governs the baseline sparsity level. For a predictor lacking neighbors in either graph, its marginal inclusion probability simplifies to $\text{expit}(\mu)$.
* $\eta_1, \eta_2 \geq 0$ are coupling parameters that dictate the strength of influence a predictor's neighbors (in $\mathbf{R}$ and $\mathbf{R}'$, respectively) exert on its inclusion probability.
* $Z(\mu, \eta_1, \eta_2)$ is the intractable normalizing constant of the distribution.

**Coupling Parameter Prior Specification:**
Within the package implementation, each coupling parameter is bounded by a user-specified upper limit (`eta1_sd`, `eta2_sd`) and sampled on a scaled Beta domain:
$$
\eta_i = \eta_{i,\max} u_i,\quad u_i \sim \text{Beta}(e, f), \quad i=1,2.
$$
Users can utilize the helper function `phase_transition()` to determine practically stable values for $\eta_{i,\max}$ prior to fitting the full model.

## Estimating Unknown Adjacency: Bayesian GGM

In scenarios where the secondary adjacency $\mathbf{R}'$ is unknown, we model the predictors as $\mathbf{X}_k \sim \mathcal{N}_p(\mathbf{0}, \boldsymbol{\Omega}^{-1})$ and impose a continuous spike-and-slab prior (SSVS) on the precision matrix $\boldsymbol{\Omega}$, following @WH2015:
$$
p(\boldsymbol{\Omega} \mid \mathbf{R}', \theta) \propto \prod_{i < j} \mathcal{N}\!\left(\omega_{ij} \mid 0,\, v^2_{r'_{ij}}\right) \prod_i \text{Exp}\!\left(\omega_{ii} \mid \tfrac{\lambda}{2}\right) \cdot \mathbf{1}_{\boldsymbol{\Omega} \in M^+}
$$
where the variance $v_{r'_{ij}}$ equals $v_0$ (spike) if $r'_{ij} = 0$, and $v_1$ (slab) if $r'_{ij} = 1$. The edge indicators are updated according to the posterior probability:
$$
\Pr(r'_{ij} = 1 \mid \boldsymbol{\Omega}) = \frac{\pi\, \mathcal{N}(\omega_{ij} \mid 0, v_1^2)}{\pi\, \mathcal{N}(\omega_{ij} \mid 0, v_1^2) + (1-\pi)\, \mathcal{N}(\omega_{ij} \mid 0, v_0^2)}
$$
## Estimating Unknown Adjacency: Graphical Lasso

As an alternative to the fully Bayesian estimation of the structural network, the secondary adjacency matrix $\mathbf{R}'$ can be estimated empirically from the continuous design matrix $\mathbf{X}$ prior to MCMC execution using the graphical lasso (glasso). Assuming the predictors follow a multivariate normal distribution, the glasso estimates the sparse precision matrix $\boldsymbol{\Omega}$ by maximizing the $L_1$-penalized log-likelihood:
$$
\hat{\boldsymbol{\Omega}} = \arg\min_{\boldsymbol{\Omega} \succ 0} \left( \text{tr}(\mathbf{S}\boldsymbol{\Omega}) - \log|\boldsymbol{\Omega}| + \lambda \sum_{i \neq j} |\omega_{ij}| \right)
$$
where $\mathbf{S}$ is the empirical sample covariance matrix and $\lambda > 0$ is a regularization parameter governing the sparsity of the resulting network. The adjacency matrix is subsequently defined by the deterministic inclusion of nonzero off-diagonal elements: $r'_{ij} = 1$ if $\hat{\omega}_{ij} \neq 0$, and $0$ otherwise.

Within the **BVS.DAdj** pipeline, this initialization is natively implemented utilizing the **huge** R package. The package efficiently computes a solution path over a grid of candidate $\lambda$ values and selects the optimal precision matrix guided by one of two robust model selection criteria:

1. **Extended Bayesian Information Criterion (EBIC):** Modifies the standard BIC by incorporating an additional penalty scaled to the complexity of the model space. This adjustment is specifically calibrated to control the false discovery rate in high-dimensional topologies where $p \gg n$.
2. **Rotation Information Criterion (RIC):** A non-parametric, data-driven methodology that establishes the optimal $\lambda$ by executing random rotations of the empirical data, thereby deriving a baseline null distribution to tightly control spurious edge formation without parametric assumptions.
# MCMC Sampling Strategies {#sec-mcmc}

## Metropolis-Hastings Sampler

The Metropolis-Hastings sampler, invoked via `bvs_mh()`, utilizes random-walk proposals for the continuous parameters $\boldsymbol{\beta}$, $\alpha$, and $\sigma^2$. For the binary indicators $\gamma_j$, it employs a component-wise add/delete move. Specifically, a flip $\gamma_j^* = 1 - \gamma_j$ is proposed, and a corresponding $\beta_j^*$ is drawn from the spike-and-slab prior. This move is accepted with probability $\min(1, \exp(\Delta))$, where:
$$
\Delta = \ell(\boldsymbol{\beta}^*, \boldsymbol{\gamma}^*) - \ell(\boldsymbol{\beta}, \boldsymbol{\gamma}) - \frac{(\beta_j - \beta_0)^2 - (\beta_j^* - \beta_0)^2}{2\sigma^2}
$$

## Pólya-Gamma Augmented Gibbs Sampler

The Pólya-Gamma (PG) sampler, invoked via `bvs_pg()`, leverages data augmentation by introducing auxiliary variables $\omega_k \sim \text{PG}(1, \psi_k)$ where $\psi_k = \alpha + \mathbf{X}_k \boldsymbol{\beta}$. This augmentation renders the logistic likelihood conditionally Gaussian [@PG2013]. In the C++ backend, PG draws are efficiently generated via a truncated-series approximation. Subsequent block updates for $(\boldsymbol{\beta}, \alpha)$ are performed using standard Gaussian conditional forms:
$$
\boldsymbol{\beta}_\gamma \mid \cdot \sim \mathcal{N}\!\left(\mathbf{V}_\gamma \mathbf{m}_\gamma,\; \mathbf{V}_\gamma\right), \quad \mathbf{V}_\gamma = \left(\mathbf{X}_\gamma^\top \boldsymbol{\Omega}_\omega \mathbf{X}_\gamma + \sigma^{-2} \mathbf{I}\right)^{-1}
$$
where $\boldsymbol{\Omega}_\omega = \text{diag}(\omega_1, \ldots, \omega_n)$ and $\kappa_k = y_k - 1/2$.

## Möller Auxiliary Variable Update for $\eta_1, \eta_2$

Due to the intractable nature of the normalizing constant $Z(\mu, \eta_1, \eta_2)$, standard MH updates for the coupling parameters are infeasible. Following the approach of @MB2006, we introduce auxiliary variables $\boldsymbol{\omega}$ drawn via Propp-Wilson perfect simulation from the Ising model at reference parameters $(\tilde{\mu}, \tilde{\eta}_1, \tilde{\eta}_2)$. This technique yields an MH acceptance ratio where the intractable ratio $Z'/Z$ gracefully cancels out.

## Sparse High-Dimensional Variant

To accommodate high-dimensional settings where $p \gg n$, the package implements specialized sparse backends. These routines store the empirical covariance matrix $\mathbf{S} = \mathbf{X}^\top\mathbf{X}$ in a compressed sparse column (CSC) format and execute column-wise block Gibbs steps for the precision matrix. The selection indicator updates are optimized using a targeted MH scheme that cycles through `n_mh_gamma` randomly chosen variables per iteration, drastically reducing the computational footprint.

# Package Design and API {#sec-api}

## Installation

The package can be installed directly from GitHub:

```{r install, eval=FALSE}
devtools::install_github("yuy113/BVS.DAdj")
```

```{r load-pkg}
library(BVS.DAdj)
```

## User-Facing Functions

The architecture of **BVS.DAdj** revolves around two primary functions that act as dispatchers to 12 optimized C++ backends:

| Function | Sampler Methodology | Primary Application |
|:---|:---|:---|
| `bvs_mh()` | Metropolis-Hastings | Universal applicability across all adjacency types; general purpose. |
| `bvs_pg()` | Pólya-Gamma Gibbs | Universal applicability; provides superior mixing properties for moderate $p$. |

Both functions share a unified, intuitive interface:

```{r api-example, eval=FALSE}
fit <- bvs_mh(X, y,
  adj_type = c("fixed", "dual_fixed", "glasso", "glasso_fixed", "ggm", "ggm_fixed"),
  adj_fixed = NULL, # Required when specifying "fixed", "dual_fixed", "glasso_fixed", "ggm_fixed"
  adj_fixed2 = NULL, # Required specifically for "dual_fixed"
  sparse = FALSE, # Set to TRUE to trigger sparse-GGM backends for high-dimensional data
  niter = 60000, burnin = 10000, thin = 1,
  # ... additional model hyperparameters
)
```

## Supported Adjacency Types

| `adj_type` | Structural Information Source(s) | $\eta$ Parameters | Available C++ Backends |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| `"fixed"` | Single user-supplied binary matrix | 1 | MH + PG |
| `"dual_fixed"` | Two independent user-supplied binary matrices | 2 | MH + PG |
| `"glasso"` | Graphical lasso, estimated empirically from $\mathbf{X}$ | 1 | MH + PG |
| `"glasso_fixed"` | Graphical lasso + one user-supplied matrix | 2 | MH + PG |
| `"ggm"` | Bayesian GGM SSVS (dense or sparse implementations) | 1 | MH + PG × dense/sparse |
| `"ggm_fixed"` | Bayesian GGM + one user-supplied matrix | 2 | MH + PG × dense/sparse |

## Post-Processing Utilities

The package includes standard S3 methods to facilitate immediate interpretation of the posterior samples:

```{r post-process, eval=FALSE}
summary(fit) # Yields selected variables, PIPs, and posterior intervals
plot(fit) # Generates a PIP bar plot, highlighting top candidates
```

# Simulation Study 1: Moderate Dimensions ($n = 500, p = 100$) {#sec-sim1}

## Data Generation Methodology

To evaluate sampler performance, we simulated a logistic regression dataset comprising $n = 500$ observations and $p = 100$ covariates, where exactly $p^* = 10$ variables represent the true active set. We enforced a block-correlation structure ($\rho = 0.5$) among the active covariates to emulate the clustering behavior frequently observed in metabolite networks.

```{r sim1-data}
set.seed(2024)
n <- 500
p <- 100
p_star <- 10

# Construct correlation matrix: block structure for first p_star variables
Sigma <- diag(p)
for (i in 1:p_star) {
  for (j in 1:p_star) {
    if (i != j) Sigma[i, j] <- 0.5
  }
}

# Generate covariates from multivariate normal
X <- MASS::mvrnorm(n, mu = rep(0, p), Sigma = Sigma)

# True coefficients: first p_star variables have nonzero effects
beta_true <- rep(0, p)
beta_true[1:p_star] <- c(1.5, -1.2, 1.0, -0.8, 0.6, -0.5, 0.4, -0.3, 0.25, -0.2)

# Generate binary response
eta_true <- X %*% beta_true
prob_true <- plogis(eta_true)
y <- rbinom(n, 1, prob_true)

cat(sprintf(
  "Response balance: %d cases / %d controls (%.1f%%)\n",
  sum(y), sum(1 - y), 100 * mean(y)
))
```

## Adjacency Matrix Construction

To facilitate a rigorous comparison across different adjacency specifications, we constructed the following network matrices:

1.  A **true** adjacency matrix (`R_true1`), perfectly mirroring the block-correlation structure used to generate the data.
2.  An **external fixed** adjacency matrix (`R_ext50_1`), generated by randomly retaining exactly 50% of the true edges, simulating incomplete biological knowledge.
3.  A **legacy partial/noisy** matrix (`R_known`), maintained to ensure backward compatibility with earlier baseline benchmarks.

```{r sim1-adj}
# True adjacency used in simulation design (first p_star block)
R_true1 <- matrix(0L, p, p)
for (i in 1:p_star) {
  for (j in (i + 1):p_star) {
    R_true1[i, j] <- R_true1[j, i] <- 1L
  }
}
diag(R_true1) <- 0L

# External fixed adjacency: random 50% of true edges
set.seed(202401)
true_pairs1 <- which(upper.tri(R_true1) & R_true1 == 1L, arr.ind = TRUE)
n_keep1 <- ceiling(0.5 * nrow(true_pairs1))
keep_idx1 <- sample(seq_len(nrow(true_pairs1)), n_keep1)
keep_pairs1 <- true_pairs1[keep_idx1, , drop = FALSE]
R_ext50_1 <- matrix(0L, p, p)
R_ext50_1[cbind(keep_pairs1[, 1], keep_pairs1[, 2])] <- 1L
R_ext50_1[cbind(keep_pairs1[, 2], keep_pairs1[, 1])] <- 1L
diag(R_ext50_1) <- 0L

# Legacy partial/noisy adjacency from earlier version of the vignette
R_known <- matrix(0L, p, p)
# Block edges among first 8 active variables
for (i in 1:8) {
  for (j in (i + 1):8) {
    R_known[i, j] <- R_known[j, i] <- 1L
  }
}
# Add a few noise edges
set.seed(123)
noise_pairs <- matrix(sample((p_star + 1):p, 10), ncol = 2)
for (k in 1:nrow(noise_pairs)) {
  R_known[noise_pairs[k, 1], noise_pairs[k, 2]] <- 1L
  R_known[noise_pairs[k, 2], noise_pairs[k, 1]] <- 1L
}
diag(R_known) <- 0L
cat(sprintf("True adjacency edges (R_true1): %d\n", sum(R_true1) / 2))
cat(sprintf("External 50%% adjacency edges (R_ext50_1): %d\n", sum(R_ext50_1) / 2))
cat(sprintf("Legacy fixed adjacency edges (R_known): %d\n", sum(R_known) / 2))
```

## Eta/Moller Hyperparameter Grid Tuning (Simulation 1)

We tune `eta1_sd`, `eta2_sd`, and Moller hyperparameters on a fixed `PIP = 0.5` decision rule. The optimization criterion is maximum sensitivity among candidates with `FDR <= 0.05`.

```{r sim1-eta-grid-tune, cache=TRUE}
cfg_tune_sim1 <- mcmc_scale(
  full_niter = 20000L, full_burnin = 10000L, full_thin = 1L,
  quick_niter = 20L, quick_burnin = 10L, quick_thin = 1L
)
niter_tune_sim1 <- cfg_tune_sim1$niter
burnin_tune_sim1 <- cfg_tune_sim1$burnin
thin_tune_sim1 <- cfg_tune_sim1$thin

sim1_eta_coarse <- seq(0.5, 5, by = 1)
sim1_tilde_frac <- 0.2
sim1_mu_tilde <- default_hpars_sim1$mu_tilde
sim1_e_eta <- default_hpars_sim1$e_eta
sim1_f_eta <- default_hpars_sim1$f_eta

sim1_coarse_grid <- bvs_eta_grid_dual_reasonable(
  eta1_sd = sim1_eta_coarse,
  eta2_sd = sim1_eta_coarse,
  mu_tilde = sim1_mu_tilde,
  eta1_tilde_frac = sim1_tilde_frac,
  eta2_tilde_frac = sim1_tilde_frac,
  e_eta = sim1_e_eta,
  f_eta = sim1_f_eta
)
if (isTRUE(quick_check) && nrow(sim1_coarse_grid) > 6L) {
  sim1_coarse_grid <- sim1_coarse_grid[seq_len(6L), , drop = FALSE]
}

eta_tune_dir <- "eta_tuning_saved_runs"
if (!dir.exists(eta_tune_dir)) dir.create(eta_tune_dir, recursive = TRUE)

sim1_tune_file <- file.path(
  eta_tune_dir,
  sprintf("sim1_dual_fixed_tune_coarseFine_eta0p5to5_by1_pip05_fdr05_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", niter_tune_sim1, burnin_tune_sim1, thin_tune_sim1)
)

if (file.exists(sim1_tune_file)) {
  sim1_tune_pack <- readRDS(sim1_tune_file)
} else {
  sim1_tune_coarse <- bvs_tune_eta_fixed_pip(
    X = X, y = y,
    adj_type = "dual_fixed",
    sampler = "mh",
    truth_idx = seq_len(p_star),
    adj_fixed = R_known,
    adj_fixed2 = R_ext50_1,
    grid = sim1_coarse_grid,
    niter = niter_tune_sim1,
    burnin = burnin_tune_sim1,
    thin = thin_tune_sim1,
    n_mh_gamma = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 5L else 10L,
    mu = default_hpars_sim1$mu,
    nu0 = 2, sigmasq0 = 1.5, h = 1.5,
    Tmax = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 16L else 32L,
    proposal_type = 1L,
    pip_threshold_fixed = pip_threshold_fixed,
    target_fdr = sim_target_fdr,
    keep_fits = FALSE,
    verbose = FALSE,
    seed = 202611
  )

  sim1_best_coarse <- sim1_tune_coarse$best
  sim1_best_eta1 <- if (!is.null(sim1_best_coarse) && "eta1_sd" %in% names(sim1_best_coarse)) {
    as.numeric(sim1_best_coarse$eta1_sd[1])
  } else {
    default_hpars_sim1$eta1_sd
  }
  sim1_best_eta2 <- if (!is.null(sim1_best_coarse) && "eta2_sd" %in% names(sim1_best_coarse)) {
    as.numeric(sim1_best_coarse$eta2_sd[1])
  } else {
    default_hpars_sim1$eta2_sd
  }
  if (!is.finite(sim1_best_eta1)) sim1_best_eta1 <- default_hpars_sim1$eta1_sd
  if (!is.finite(sim1_best_eta2)) sim1_best_eta2 <- default_hpars_sim1$eta2_sd

  sim1_fine_eta1 <- seq(max(0.5, sim1_best_eta1 - 1), min(5, sim1_best_eta1 + 1), by = 0.25)
  sim1_fine_eta2 <- seq(max(0.5, sim1_best_eta2 - 1), min(5, sim1_best_eta2 + 1), by = 0.25)
  if (isTRUE(quick_check)) {
    sim1_fine_eta1 <- unique(round(c(max(0.5, sim1_best_eta1 - 0.5), sim1_best_eta1, min(5, sim1_best_eta1 + 0.5)), 3))
    sim1_fine_eta2 <- unique(round(c(max(0.5, sim1_best_eta2 - 0.5), sim1_best_eta2, min(5, sim1_best_eta2 + 0.5)), 3))
  }

  sim1_fine_grid <- bvs_eta_grid_dual_reasonable(
    eta1_sd = sim1_fine_eta1,
    eta2_sd = sim1_fine_eta2,
    mu_tilde = sim1_mu_tilde,
    eta1_tilde_frac = sim1_tilde_frac,
    eta2_tilde_frac = sim1_tilde_frac,
    e_eta = sim1_e_eta,
    f_eta = sim1_f_eta
  )
  if (isTRUE(quick_check) && nrow(sim1_fine_grid) > 9L) {
    sim1_fine_grid <- sim1_fine_grid[seq_len(9L), , drop = FALSE]
  }

  sim1_tune_fine <- bvs_tune_eta_fixed_pip(
    X = X, y = y,
    adj_type = "dual_fixed",
    sampler = "mh",
    truth_idx = seq_len(p_star),
    adj_fixed = R_known,
    adj_fixed2 = R_ext50_1,
    grid = sim1_fine_grid,
    niter = niter_tune_sim1,
    burnin = burnin_tune_sim1,
    thin = thin_tune_sim1,
    n_mh_gamma = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 5L else 10L,
    mu = default_hpars_sim1$mu,
    nu0 = 2, sigmasq0 = 1.5, h = 1.5,
    Tmax = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 16L else 32L,
    proposal_type = 1L,
    pip_threshold_fixed = pip_threshold_fixed,
    target_fdr = sim_target_fdr,
    keep_fits = FALSE,
    verbose = FALSE,
    seed = 202711
  )

  sim1_tune_pack <- list(
    coarse = sim1_tune_coarse,
    fine = sim1_tune_fine,
    coarse_best_eta1_sd = sim1_best_eta1,
    coarse_best_eta2_sd = sim1_best_eta2,
    fine_eta1_range = range(sim1_fine_eta1),
    fine_eta2_range = range(sim1_fine_eta2)
  )
  saveRDS(sim1_tune_pack, sim1_tune_file)
}

hpars_sim1_tune_stage <- if (!is.null(sim1_tune_pack$fine)) "fine" else "coarse"
sim1_tune <- if (!is.null(sim1_tune_pack$fine)) sim1_tune_pack$fine else sim1_tune_pack$coarse
hpars_sim1 <- coalesce_hpars_from_tune(sim1_tune, default_hpars_sim1, mu_override = default_hpars_sim1$mu)
hpars_real <- hpars_sim1
hpars_sim1_tag <- paste0("gridtune_", hpars_tag(hpars_sim1))
hpars_real_tag <- hpars_sim1_tag

sim1_tune_summary <- sim1_tune$results
if (nrow(sim1_tune_summary) > 0L) {
  sim1_tune_summary <- sim1_tune_summary[order(sim1_tune_summary$fdr, -sim1_tune_summary$sensitivity), , drop = FALSE]
  keep_cols <- c("eta1_sd", "eta2_sd", "mu_tilde", "eta1_tilde", "eta2_tilde", "e_eta", "f_eta", "sensitivity", "fdr", "selected_size")
  keep_cols <- keep_cols[keep_cols %in% names(sim1_tune_summary)]
  knitr::kable(
    head(sim1_tune_summary[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE], 8),
    digits = 3,
    caption = sprintf(
      "Simulation 1 eta/Moller tuning summary (%s stage, fixed PIP = 0.5, coarse eta grid seq(0.5, 5, 1), fine local refinement).",
      hpars_sim1_tune_stage
    )
  )
}
```

## Model Execution

We evaluated the performance of three distinct backends on this dataset:

1.  **MH with fixed adjacency** (`bvs_mh`, `adj_type = "fixed"`)
2.  **PG with fixed adjacency** (`bvs_pg`, `adj_type = "fixed"`)
3.  **PG with dense Bayesian GGM** (`bvs_pg`, `adj_type = "ggm"`)

```{r sim1-run, cache=TRUE}
cfg_sim1 <- mcmc_scale(
  full_niter = 5000L, full_burnin = 2500L, full_thin = 1L,
  quick_niter = 5L, quick_burnin = 2L, quick_thin = 1L
)
niter_sim <- cfg_sim1$niter
burnin_sim <- cfg_sim1$burnin
thin_sim <- cfg_sim1$thin

# --- MH with fixed adjacency ---
t_mh <- system.time({
  fit_mh <- bvs_mh(X, y,
    adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known,
    niter = niter_sim, burnin = burnin_sim,
    mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim,
    eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
    e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
    mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
  )
})

# --- PG with fixed adjacency ---
t_pg <- system.time({
  fit_pg <- bvs_pg(X, y,
    adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known,
    niter = niter_sim, burnin = burnin_sim,
    mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim,
    eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
    e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
    mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
  )
})

# --- PG with dense Bayesian GGM ---
t_pg_ggm <- system.time({
  fit_pg_ggm <- bvs_pg(X, y,
    adj_type = "ggm", sparse = FALSE,
    niter = niter_sim, burnin = burnin_sim,
    mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim,
    eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
    e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
    mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
  )
})
```

## Computational Profiling

Table 1 summarizes the computational efficiency of the respective MCMC strategies.

```{r sim1-timing}
timing_df <- data.frame(
  Method = c("MH (fixed adj)", "PG (fixed adj)", "PG (Bayesian GGM)"),
  Elapsed_sec = round(c(t_mh["elapsed"], t_pg["elapsed"], t_pg_ggm["elapsed"]), 1),
  Iter_per_sec = round(niter_sim / c(t_mh["elapsed"], t_pg["elapsed"], t_pg_ggm["elapsed"]), 0)
)
knitr::kable(
  timing_df,
  caption = sprintf(
    "Running time comparison ($n = 500, p = 100$, %s post-burn-in iterations, burn-in %s).",
    format(niter_sim, big.mark = ","),
    format(burnin_sim, big.mark = ",")
  ),
  col.names = c("Method", "Elapsed (sec)", "Iter/sec")
)
```

## MCMC Convergence Diagnostics

### Trace and Running Mean Analyses

```{r sim1-trace, fig.height=8, fig.width=8, fig.cap="Trace plots of selected regression coefficients (beta_1 through beta_4) across samplers. Dashed red lines denote true data-generating values."}
par(mfrow = c(3, 4), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1), mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0))
vars_to_plot <- 1:4

for (method_name in c("MH (fixed)", "PG (fixed)", "PG (GGM)")) {
  fit_cur <- switch(method_name,
    "MH (fixed)" = fit_mh,
    "PG (fixed)" = fit_pg,
    "PG (GGM)"   = fit_pg_ggm
  )
  for (j in vars_to_plot) {
    plot(fit_cur$beta[, j],
      type = "l", col = "steelblue",
      main = paste0(method_name, ": beta_", j),
      xlab = "Iteration", ylab = expression(beta[j]),
      cex.main = 0.85
    )
    abline(h = beta_true[j], col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 1.5)
  }
}
```

```{r sim1-runmean, fig.height=6, fig.width=8, fig.cap="Running means of coefficients beta_1 and beta_2, affirming convergence to the true parameters (dashed red lines)."}
running_mean <- function(x) cumsum(x) / seq_along(x)

par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1), mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0))
for (j in 1:2) {
  for (method_name in c("MH (fixed)", "PG (fixed)", "PG (GGM)")) {
    fit_cur <- switch(method_name,
      "MH (fixed)" = fit_mh,
      "PG (fixed)" = fit_pg,
      "PG (GGM)"   = fit_pg_ggm
    )
    rm_vals <- running_mean(fit_cur$beta[, j])
    plot(rm_vals,
      type = "l", col = "darkgreen", lwd = 1.2,
      main = paste0(method_name, ": beta_", j),
      xlab = "Iteration", ylab = "Running mean",
      cex.main = 0.85
    )
    abline(h = beta_true[j], col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 1.5)
  }
}
```

### Autocorrelation Assessment

```{r sim1-acf, fig.height=4, fig.width=8, fig.cap="Autocorrelation functions (ACF) for beta_1, illustrating the relative mixing efficiencies of the sampling strategies."}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1), mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0))
safe_acf_plot(fit_mh$beta[, 1], lag.max = 50, main = "MH (fixed): beta_1", col = "steelblue")
safe_acf_plot(fit_pg$beta[, 1], lag.max = 50, main = "PG (fixed): beta_1", col = "steelblue")
safe_acf_plot(fit_pg_ggm$beta[, 1], lag.max = 50, main = "PG (GGM): beta_1", col = "steelblue")
```

### Gelman-Rubin Diagnostics

To formally assess convergence, we executed dual chains across distinct random seeds and computed the Potential Scale Reduction Factor (PSRF).

```{r sim1-gelman, cache=TRUE, eval=TRUE}
vars_gr1 <- 1:4
seed_sim1 <- c(20261101L, 20261102L)
cfg_sim1_gr <- mcmc_scale(
  full_niter = min(600L, niter_sim),
  full_burnin = min(250L, burnin_sim),
  full_thin = thin_sim,
  quick_niter = 120L,
  quick_burnin = 50L,
  quick_thin = 1L
)
niter_sim1_gr <- cfg_sim1_gr$niter
burnin_sim1_gr <- cfg_sim1_gr$burnin
thin_sim1_gr <- cfg_sim1_gr$thin

set.seed(seed_sim1[1])
fit_mh_gr1 <- bvs_mh(
  X, y,
  adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known,
  niter = niter_sim1_gr, burnin = burnin_sim1_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim1_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
)
set.seed(seed_sim1[2])
fit_mh_gr2 <- bvs_mh(
  X, y,
  adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known,
  niter = niter_sim1_gr, burnin = burnin_sim1_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim1_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
)

set.seed(seed_sim1[1])
fit_pg_gr1 <- bvs_pg(
  X, y,
  adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known,
  niter = niter_sim1_gr, burnin = burnin_sim1_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim1_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
)
set.seed(seed_sim1[2])
fit_pg_gr2 <- bvs_pg(
  X, y,
  adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known,
  niter = niter_sim1_gr, burnin = burnin_sim1_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim1_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
)

set.seed(seed_sim1[1])
fit_pg_ggm_gr1 <- bvs_pg(
  X, y,
  adj_type = "ggm", sparse = FALSE,
  niter = niter_sim1_gr, burnin = burnin_sim1_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim1_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
)
set.seed(seed_sim1[2])
fit_pg_ggm_gr2 <- bvs_pg(
  X, y,
  adj_type = "ggm", sparse = FALSE,
  niter = niter_sim1_gr, burnin = burnin_sim1_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim1$mu, thin = thin_sim1_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
)

gelman_sim1 <- list(
  "MH (fixed adj)" = safe_gelman_df(list(fit_mh_gr1, fit_mh_gr2), vars = vars_gr1, autoburnin = FALSE),
  "PG (fixed adj)" = safe_gelman_df(list(fit_pg_gr1, fit_pg_gr2), vars = vars_gr1, autoburnin = FALSE),
  "PG (Bayesian GGM)" = safe_gelman_df(list(fit_pg_ggm_gr1, fit_pg_ggm_gr2), vars = vars_gr1, autoburnin = FALSE)
)

gelman_sim1_summary <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(gelman_sim1), function(mn) {
  gelman_method_summary(gelman_sim1[[mn]], mn)
}))

gelman_sim1_detail <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(gelman_sim1), function(mn) {
  df <- gelman_sim1[[mn]]
  data.frame(
    Method = mn,
    Parameter = df$Parameter,
    Point_Rhat = round(df$Point_est, 3),
    Upper_CI_Rhat = round(df$Upper_CI, 3),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

knitr::kable(
  gelman_sim1_summary,
  caption = "Simulation 1 Gelman-Rubin convergence diagnostics using dual chains."
)
knitr::kable(
  gelman_sim1_detail,
  caption = "Simulation 1 detailed PSRF values for intercept and active coefficients."
)
```

```{r sim1-gelman-trace-2chain, eval=TRUE, fig.height=10, fig.width=12, fig.cap="Simulation 1: two-chain trace plots for representative coefficients across Gelman-Rubin diagnostic runs."}
beta_idx_gr1_trace <- unique(c(1L, 2L, 5L, min(p, p_star + 5L)))
par(
  mfrow = c(3, length(beta_idx_gr1_trace)),
  mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1),
  mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0)
)
plot_two_chain_trace_panels(fit_mh_gr1, fit_mh_gr2, beta_idx_gr1_trace, "MH (fixed)")
plot_two_chain_trace_panels(fit_pg_gr1, fit_pg_gr2, beta_idx_gr1_trace, "PG (fixed)")
plot_two_chain_trace_panels(fit_pg_ggm_gr1, fit_pg_ggm_gr2, beta_idx_gr1_trace, "PG (Bayesian GGM)")
```

## Variable Selection Performance

### Posterior Inclusion Probabilities

```{r sim1-pip, fig.height=5, fig.width=9, fig.cap="Posterior inclusion probabilities (PIPs) across the 100 candidate variables. Red bars highlight the truly active signals (variables 1-10). The dashed line delineates the conventional 0.5 threshold for inclusion."}
compute_pip <- function(fit) {
  if (!is.null(fit$gamma_pip)) {
    return(as.numeric(fit$gamma_pip))
  }
  if (is.matrix(fit$gamma)) {
    return(colMeans(fit$gamma))
  }
  if (is.list(fit$gamma)) {
    p <- fit$p
    n_save <- length(fit$gamma)
    pip <- numeric(p)
    if (n_save > 0) {
      for (s in seq_len(n_save)) {
        idx <- as.integer(fit$gamma[[s]])
        idx <- idx[idx >= 1L & idx <= p]
        if (length(idx) > 0) pip[idx] <- pip[idx] + 1
      }
      pip <- pip / n_save
    }
    return(pip)
  }
  stop("No gamma samples available for PIP computation.")
}

get_gamma_matrix <- function(fit) {
  if (is.matrix(fit$gamma)) {
    return(fit$gamma)
  }
  if (is.list(fit$gamma)) {
    p <- fit$p
    n_save <- length(fit$gamma)
    G <- matrix(0, n_save, p)
    if (n_save > 0) {
      for (s in seq_len(n_save)) {
        idx <- as.integer(fit$gamma[[s]])
        idx <- idx[idx >= 1L & idx <= p]
        if (length(idx) > 0) G[s, idx] <- 1
      }
    }
    return(G)
  }
  stop("No gamma samples available.")
}

get_beta_matrix <- function(fit) {
  if (is.matrix(fit$beta)) {
    return(fit$beta)
  }
  if (is.list(fit$beta)) {
    p <- fit$p
    n_save <- length(fit$beta)
    B <- matrix(0, n_save, p)
    if (n_save > 0) {
      for (s in seq_len(n_save)) {
        bm <- fit$beta[[s]]
        if (is.null(bm) || length(bm) == 0) next
        bm <- as.matrix(bm)
        if (nrow(bm) > 0 && ncol(bm) >= 2) {
          idx <- as.integer(bm[, 1]) # C++ sparse backends emit 1-based indices
          valid <- idx >= 1L & idx <= p
          B[s, idx[valid]] <- as.numeric(bm[valid, 2])
        }
      }
    }
    return(B)
  }
  stop("No beta samples available.")
}

model_size_chain <- function(fit) {
  if (is.matrix(fit$gamma)) {
    return(rowSums(fit$gamma))
  }
  if (is.list(fit$gamma)) {
    return(vapply(fit$gamma, length, integer(1)))
  }
  stop("No gamma samples available.")
}

pip_mh <- compute_pip(fit_mh)
pip_pg <- compute_pip(fit_pg)
pip_ggm <- compute_pip(fit_pg_ggm)

par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))
cols <- ifelse(1:p <= p_star, "firebrick", "grey60")

barplot(pip_mh,
  col = cols, border = NA, main = "MH (fixed adj)",
  xlab = "Variable index", ylab = "PIP", ylim = c(0, 1)
)
abline(h = pip_threshold_fixed, lty = 2, col = "black")

barplot(pip_pg,
  col = cols, border = NA, main = "PG (fixed adj)",
  xlab = "Variable index", ylab = "PIP", ylim = c(0, 1)
)
abline(h = pip_threshold_fixed, lty = 2, col = "black")

barplot(pip_ggm,
  col = cols, border = NA, main = "PG (Bayesian GGM)",
  xlab = "Variable index", ylab = "PIP", ylim = c(0, 1)
)
abline(h = pip_threshold_fixed, lty = 2, col = "black")
```

### Selection Accuracy

```{r sim1-metrics}
compute_metrics_from_selected <- function(selected, truth_idx, p) {
  selected <- sort(unique(as.integer(selected)))
  selected <- selected[selected >= 1L & selected <= p]

  truth_idx <- sort(unique(as.integer(truth_idx)))
  truth_idx <- truth_idx[truth_idx >= 1L & truth_idx <= p]

  tp <- sum(selected %in% truth_idx)
  fp <- length(selected) - tp
  fn <- length(truth_idx) - tp
  sensitivity <- if (length(truth_idx) > 0L) tp / length(truth_idx) else NA_real_
  fdr <- if (length(selected) > 0L) fp / length(selected) else 0
  f1 <- if (tp + fp + fn > 0L) 2 * tp / (2 * tp + fp + fn) else 0

  data.frame(
    TP = tp, FP = fp, FN = fn,
    Sensitivity = round(sensitivity, 3),
    FDR = round(fdr, 3),
    F1 = round(f1, 3),
    N_selected = length(selected)
  )
}

compute_metrics <- function(pip, threshold = 0.5, p_star = 10) {
  selected <- which(pip >= threshold)
  compute_metrics_from_selected(selected, truth_idx = seq_len(p_star), p = length(pip))
}

metrics_at_threshold <- function(pip, truth_idx, threshold) {
  p <- length(pip)
  selected <- which(pip >= threshold)
  compute_metrics_from_selected(selected, truth_idx = truth_idx, p = p)
}

compute_metrics_fixed_pip <- function(pip, p_star = 10, pip_threshold = 0.5, target_fdr = 0.05) {
  truth_idx <- seq_len(p_star)
  out <- metrics_at_threshold(pip, truth_idx = truth_idx, threshold = pip_threshold)
  out$PIP_threshold <- round(pip_threshold, 3)
  out$Target_FDR <- target_fdr
  out
}

metrics <- rbind(
  cbind(Method = "MH (fixed adj)", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip_mh, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "PG (fixed adj)", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip_pg, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "PG (Bayesian GGM)", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip_ggm, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr))
)
knitr::kable(metrics,
  caption = sprintf(
    "Variable selection performance under fixed PIP cutoff %.2f and target FDR <= %.2f ($n = 500, p = 100$).",
    pip_threshold_fixed, sim_target_fdr
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

### Edge-Coupling Parameter Traces

```{r sim1-eta, fig.height=4, fig.width=8, fig.cap="MCMC trace plots for the network coupling parameter eta across sampling configurations."}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1), mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0))
if (!is.null(fit_mh$eta1)) {
  plot(fit_mh$eta1,
    type = "l", col = "purple",
    main = "MH: eta1", xlab = "Iteration", ylab = expression(eta)
  )
}
if (!is.null(fit_pg$eta1)) {
  plot(fit_pg$eta1,
    type = "l", col = "purple",
    main = "PG: eta1", xlab = "Iteration", ylab = expression(eta)
  )
}
if (!is.null(fit_pg_ggm$eta1)) {
  plot(fit_pg_ggm$eta1,
    type = "l", col = "purple",
    main = "PG GGM: eta1", xlab = "Iteration", ylab = expression(eta)
  )
}
```

## Comparative Analysis of Network Priors (5 Methods)

To rigorously dissect the impact of varying the structural information source, we executed five MH sampler configurations on the same synthetic dataset. For routines requiring fixed inputs, we utilized `R_ext50_1` (retaining 50% of the generating edges):

1.  `adj_type = "fixed"` (External biological knowledge only).
2.  `adj_type = "ggm"` (Fully data-driven via Bayesian SSVS).
3.  `adj_type = "glasso"` (Fully data-driven via graphical lasso).
4.  `adj_type = "glasso_fixed"` (Dual network: biological + glasso).
5.  `adj_type = "ggm_fixed"` (Dual network: biological + Bayesian GGM).

```{r sim1-adjtype-run, cache=TRUE}
sim1_adj_dir <- "sim1_adjtype_saved_runs"
if (!dir.exists(sim1_adj_dir)) dir.create(sim1_adj_dir, recursive = TRUE)

niter_adj1 <- niter_sim
burnin_adj1 <- burnin_sim
thin_adj1 <- thin_sim
if (!exists("hpars_sim1_tag", inherits = TRUE)) {
  hpars_sim1_tag <- paste0("gridtune_", hpars_tag(hpars_sim1))
}
sim1_hp_tag <- hpars_sim1_tag

sim1_f_fixed <- file.path(sim1_adj_dir, sprintf("sim1_fixed50_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim1_hp_tag, niter_adj1, burnin_adj1, thin_adj1))
sim1_f_ggm <- file.path(sim1_adj_dir, sprintf("sim1_ggm_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim1_hp_tag, niter_adj1, burnin_adj1, thin_adj1))
sim1_f_gls <- file.path(sim1_adj_dir, sprintf("sim1_glasso_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim1_hp_tag, niter_adj1, burnin_adj1, thin_adj1))
sim1_f_gf <- file.path(sim1_adj_dir, sprintf("sim1_glasso_fixed_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim1_hp_tag, niter_adj1, burnin_adj1, thin_adj1))
sim1_f_ggmf <- file.path(sim1_adj_dir, sprintf("sim1_ggm_fixed_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim1_hp_tag, niter_adj1, burnin_adj1, thin_adj1))

if (file.exists(sim1_f_fixed)) {
  o <- readRDS(sim1_f_fixed)
  fit1_fixed50 <- o$fit
  t1_fixed50 <- o$time
} else {
  t1_fixed50 <- system.time({
    fit1_fixed50 <- bvs_mh(
      X, y,
      adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_ext50_1,
      niter = niter_adj1, burnin = burnin_adj1, thin = thin_adj1,
      mu = hpars_sim1$mu,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit1_fixed50, time = t1_fixed50), sim1_f_fixed)
}

if (file.exists(sim1_f_ggm)) {
  o <- readRDS(sim1_f_ggm)
  fit1_ggm <- o$fit
  t1_ggm <- o$time
} else {
  t1_ggm <- system.time({
    fit1_ggm <- bvs_mh(
      X, y,
      adj_type = "ggm", sparse = FALSE,
      niter = niter_adj1, burnin = burnin_adj1, thin = thin_adj1,
      mu = hpars_sim1$mu,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit1_ggm, time = t1_ggm), sim1_f_ggm)
}

if (file.exists(sim1_f_gls)) {
  o <- readRDS(sim1_f_gls)
  fit1_glasso <- o$fit
  t1_glasso <- o$time
} else {
  t1_glasso <- system.time({
    fit1_glasso <- bvs_mh(
      X, y,
      adj_type = "glasso", glasso_criterion = "ebic",
      niter = niter_adj1, burnin = burnin_adj1, thin = thin_adj1,
      mu = hpars_sim1$mu,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit1_glasso, time = t1_glasso), sim1_f_gls)
}

if (file.exists(sim1_f_gf)) {
  o <- readRDS(sim1_f_gf)
  fit1_glasso_fixed <- o$fit
  t1_glasso_fixed <- o$time
} else {
  t1_glasso_fixed <- system.time({
    fit1_glasso_fixed <- bvs_mh(
      X, y,
      adj_type = "glasso_fixed", adj_fixed = R_ext50_1,
      glasso_criterion = "ebic",
      niter = niter_adj1, burnin = burnin_adj1, thin = thin_adj1,
      mu = hpars_sim1$mu,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd, eta2_sd = hpars_sim1$eta2_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde,
      eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde, eta2_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta2_tilde
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit1_glasso_fixed, time = t1_glasso_fixed), sim1_f_gf)
}

if (file.exists(sim1_f_ggmf)) {
  o <- readRDS(sim1_f_ggmf)
  fit1_ggm_fixed <- o$fit
  t1_ggm_fixed <- o$time
} else {
  t1_ggm_fixed <- system.time({
    fit1_ggm_fixed <- bvs_mh(
      X, y,
      adj_type = "ggm_fixed", adj_fixed = R_ext50_1, sparse = FALSE,
      niter = niter_adj1, burnin = burnin_adj1, thin = thin_adj1,
      mu = hpars_sim1$mu,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim1$eta1_sd, eta2_sd = hpars_sim1$eta2_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim1$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim1$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim1$mu_tilde,
      eta1_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta1_tilde, eta2_tilde = hpars_sim1$eta2_tilde
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit1_ggm_fixed, time = t1_ggm_fixed), sim1_f_ggmf)
}
```

```{r sim1-adjtype-timing}
timing_adj1 <- data.frame(
  Method = c("Fixed (50% true edges)", "GGM", "Glasso", "Glasso + Fixed", "GGM + Fixed"),
  Elapsed_sec = round(c(
    t1_fixed50["elapsed"], t1_ggm["elapsed"], t1_glasso["elapsed"],
    t1_glasso_fixed["elapsed"], t1_ggm_fixed["elapsed"]
  ), 2),
  Iter_per_sec = round(niter_adj1 / c(
    t1_fixed50["elapsed"], t1_ggm["elapsed"], t1_glasso["elapsed"],
    t1_glasso_fixed["elapsed"], t1_ggm_fixed["elapsed"]
  ), 1)
)
knitr::kable(
  timing_adj1,
  caption = "Computational benchmarking for the five network configurations (MH sampler).",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r sim1-adjtype-pip, fig.height=8, fig.width=10, fig.cap="Comparative PIP profiles across all five adjacency-prior structures."}
pip1_fixed50 <- compute_pip(fit1_fixed50)
pip1_ggm <- compute_pip(fit1_ggm)
pip1_glasso <- compute_pip(fit1_glasso)
pip1_gf <- compute_pip(fit1_glasso_fixed)
pip1_ggmf <- compute_pip(fit1_ggm_fixed)

show_vars1 <- unique(c(
  1:40,
  which(pip1_fixed50 > 0.1),
  which(pip1_ggm > 0.1),
  which(pip1_glasso > 0.1),
  which(pip1_gf > 0.1),
  which(pip1_ggmf > 0.1)
))
show_vars1 <- sort(show_vars1)
cols1 <- ifelse(show_vars1 <= p_star, "firebrick", "grey65")

pip_list1 <- list(
  "Fixed (50% true)" = pip1_fixed50,
  "GGM" = pip1_ggm,
  "Glasso" = pip1_glasso,
  "Glasso + Fixed" = pip1_gf,
  "GGM + Fixed" = pip1_ggmf
)

par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))
for (mn in names(pip_list1)) {
  barplot(pip_list1[[mn]][show_vars1],
    col = cols1, border = NA, ylim = c(0, 1),
    main = mn, xlab = "Variable", ylab = "PIP",
    names.arg = show_vars1, las = 2, cex.names = 0.5
  )
  abline(h = pip_threshold_fixed, lty = 2)
}
plot.new()
```

```{r sim1-adjtype-metrics}
metrics_adj1 <- rbind(
  cbind(Method = "Fixed (50% true)", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip1_fixed50, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "GGM", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip1_ggm, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "Glasso", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip1_glasso, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "Glasso + Fixed", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip1_gf, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "GGM + Fixed", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip1_ggmf, p_star = p_star, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr))
)
knitr::kable(
  metrics_adj1,
  caption = sprintf(
    "Selection accuracy metrics comparing the five adjacency-prior methodologies under fixed PIP cutoff %.2f (target FDR <= %.2f).",
    pip_threshold_fixed, sim_target_fdr
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

# Simulation Study 2: High-Dimensional Sparse Setting ($p \gg n$) {#sec-sim2}

## Data Generation Methodology

To stress-test the algorithm's scalability, we simulated a high-dimensional scenario featuring $n = 200$ and $p = 1000$. The active set comprises $p^* = 15$ variables exhibiting a decaying effect size pattern.

```{r sim2-data}
set.seed(2025)
n2 <- 200
p2 <- 1000
p_star2 <- 15

# Independent covariates (sparse regime)
X2 <- matrix(rnorm(n2 * p2), n2, p2)

# Add mild correlation within the first 15 active variables
rho2 <- 0.3
for (i in 1:p_star2) {
  for (j in 1:p_star2) {
    if (i != j) {
      X2[, j] <- rho2 * X2[, i] + sqrt(1 - rho2^2) * X2[, j]
    }
  }
}

# True coefficients with decaying magnitudes
beta_true2 <- rep(0, p2)
signal_sizes <- seq(1.5, 0.3, length.out = p_star2)
signs <- rep(c(1, -1), length.out = p_star2)
beta_true2[1:p_star2] <- signs * signal_sizes

# Generate binary response
eta_true2 <- X2 %*% beta_true2
prob_true2 <- plogis(eta_true2)
y2 <- rbinom(n2, 1, prob_true2)
cat(sprintf("Response balance: %d / %d (%.1f%%)\n", sum(y2), n2 - sum(y2), 100 * mean(y2)))
```

## Adjacency Matrices

```{r sim2-adj}
# True adjacency used by simulation design: fully connected active block
R_true2 <- matrix(0L, p2, p2)
for (i in 1:p_star2) {
  for (j in (i + 1):p_star2) {
    R_true2[i, j] <- R_true2[j, i] <- 1L
  }
}
diag(R_true2) <- 0L

# External fixed adjacency: random 50% of true edges
set.seed(202502)
true_pairs2 <- which(upper.tri(R_true2) & R_true2 == 1L, arr.ind = TRUE)
n_keep2 <- ceiling(0.5 * nrow(true_pairs2))
keep_idx2 <- sample(seq_len(nrow(true_pairs2)), n_keep2)
keep_pairs2 <- true_pairs2[keep_idx2, , drop = FALSE]
R_ext50_2 <- matrix(0L, p2, p2)
if (nrow(keep_pairs2) > 0L) {
  R_ext50_2[cbind(keep_pairs2[, 1], keep_pairs2[, 2])] <- 1L
  R_ext50_2[cbind(keep_pairs2[, 2], keep_pairs2[, 1])] <- 1L
}
diag(R_ext50_2) <- 0L

# Legacy fixed adjacency used by the original 3-method benchmark
R_known2 <- matrix(0L, p2, p2)
for (i in 1:10) {
  for (j in (i + 1):min(10, p2)) {
    R_known2[i, j] <- R_known2[j, i] <- 1L
  }
}
diag(R_known2) <- 0L

cat(sprintf("True adjacency edges (R_true2): %d\n", sum(R_true2) / 2))
cat(sprintf("External 50%% adjacency edges (R_ext50_2): %d\n", sum(R_ext50_2) / 2))
cat(sprintf("Legacy fixed adjacency edges (R_known2): %d\n", sum(R_known2) / 2))
```

## Eta/Moller Hyperparameter Grid Tuning (Simulation 2)

As in Simulation 1, we tune dual-eta and Moller hyperparameters using fixed `PIP = 0.5` and select the candidate with maximal sensitivity under `FDR <= 0.05`.

```{r sim2-eta-grid-tune, cache=TRUE}
cfg_tune_sim2 <- mcmc_scale(
  full_niter = 20000L, full_burnin = 10000L, full_thin = 1L,
  quick_niter = 20L, quick_burnin = 10L, quick_thin = 1L
)
niter_tune_sim2 <- cfg_tune_sim2$niter
burnin_tune_sim2 <- cfg_tune_sim2$burnin
thin_tune_sim2 <- cfg_tune_sim2$thin

sim2_eta_coarse <- seq(0.5, 5, by = 1)
sim2_tilde_frac <- 0.2
sim2_mu_tilde <- default_hpars_sim2$mu_tilde
sim2_e_eta <- default_hpars_sim2$e_eta
sim2_f_eta <- default_hpars_sim2$f_eta

sim2_coarse_grid <- bvs_eta_grid_dual_reasonable(
  eta1_sd = sim2_eta_coarse,
  eta2_sd = sim2_eta_coarse,
  mu_tilde = sim2_mu_tilde,
  eta1_tilde_frac = sim2_tilde_frac,
  eta2_tilde_frac = sim2_tilde_frac,
  e_eta = sim2_e_eta,
  f_eta = sim2_f_eta
)
if (isTRUE(quick_check) && nrow(sim2_coarse_grid) > 6L) {
  sim2_coarse_grid <- sim2_coarse_grid[seq_len(6L), , drop = FALSE]
}

eta_tune_dir <- "eta_tuning_saved_runs"
if (!dir.exists(eta_tune_dir)) dir.create(eta_tune_dir, recursive = TRUE)

sim2_tune_file <- file.path(
  eta_tune_dir,
  sprintf("sim2_dual_fixed_tune_coarseFine_eta0p5to5_by1_pip05_fdr05_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", niter_tune_sim2, burnin_tune_sim2, thin_tune_sim2)
)

if (file.exists(sim2_tune_file)) {
  sim2_tune_pack <- readRDS(sim2_tune_file)
} else {
  sim2_tune_coarse <- bvs_tune_eta_fixed_pip(
    X = X2, y = y2,
    adj_type = "dual_fixed",
    sampler = "mh",
    truth_idx = seq_len(p_star2),
    adj_fixed = R_known2,
    adj_fixed2 = R_ext50_2,
    grid = sim2_coarse_grid,
    niter = niter_tune_sim2,
    burnin = burnin_tune_sim2,
    thin = thin_tune_sim2,
    n_mh_gamma = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 5L else 50L,
    mu = default_hpars_sim2$mu,
    nu0 = 2, sigmasq0 = 1.5, h = 1.5,
    Tmax = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 16L else 32L,
    proposal_type = 1L,
    pip_threshold_fixed = pip_threshold_fixed,
    target_fdr = sim_target_fdr,
    keep_fits = FALSE,
    verbose = FALSE,
    seed = 202612
  )

  sim2_best_coarse <- sim2_tune_coarse$best
  sim2_best_eta1 <- if (!is.null(sim2_best_coarse) && "eta1_sd" %in% names(sim2_best_coarse)) {
    as.numeric(sim2_best_coarse$eta1_sd[1])
  } else {
    default_hpars_sim2$eta1_sd
  }
  sim2_best_eta2 <- if (!is.null(sim2_best_coarse) && "eta2_sd" %in% names(sim2_best_coarse)) {
    as.numeric(sim2_best_coarse$eta2_sd[1])
  } else {
    default_hpars_sim2$eta2_sd
  }
  if (!is.finite(sim2_best_eta1)) sim2_best_eta1 <- default_hpars_sim2$eta1_sd
  if (!is.finite(sim2_best_eta2)) sim2_best_eta2 <- default_hpars_sim2$eta2_sd

  sim2_fine_eta1 <- seq(max(0.5, sim2_best_eta1 - 1), min(5, sim2_best_eta1 + 1), by = 0.25)
  sim2_fine_eta2 <- seq(max(0.5, sim2_best_eta2 - 1), min(5, sim2_best_eta2 + 1), by = 0.25)
  if (isTRUE(quick_check)) {
    sim2_fine_eta1 <- unique(round(c(max(0.5, sim2_best_eta1 - 0.5), sim2_best_eta1, min(5, sim2_best_eta1 + 0.5)), 3))
    sim2_fine_eta2 <- unique(round(c(max(0.5, sim2_best_eta2 - 0.5), sim2_best_eta2, min(5, sim2_best_eta2 + 0.5)), 3))
  }

  sim2_fine_grid <- bvs_eta_grid_dual_reasonable(
    eta1_sd = sim2_fine_eta1,
    eta2_sd = sim2_fine_eta2,
    mu_tilde = sim2_mu_tilde,
    eta1_tilde_frac = sim2_tilde_frac,
    eta2_tilde_frac = sim2_tilde_frac,
    e_eta = sim2_e_eta,
    f_eta = sim2_f_eta
  )
  if (isTRUE(quick_check) && nrow(sim2_fine_grid) > 9L) {
    sim2_fine_grid <- sim2_fine_grid[seq_len(9L), , drop = FALSE]
  }

  sim2_tune_fine <- bvs_tune_eta_fixed_pip(
    X = X2, y = y2,
    adj_type = "dual_fixed",
    sampler = "mh",
    truth_idx = seq_len(p_star2),
    adj_fixed = R_known2,
    adj_fixed2 = R_ext50_2,
    grid = sim2_fine_grid,
    niter = niter_tune_sim2,
    burnin = burnin_tune_sim2,
    thin = thin_tune_sim2,
    n_mh_gamma = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 5L else 50L,
    mu = default_hpars_sim2$mu,
    nu0 = 2, sigmasq0 = 1.5, h = 1.5,
    Tmax = if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 16L else 32L,
    proposal_type = 1L,
    pip_threshold_fixed = pip_threshold_fixed,
    target_fdr = sim_target_fdr,
    keep_fits = FALSE,
    verbose = FALSE,
    seed = 202712
  )

  sim2_tune_pack <- list(
    coarse = sim2_tune_coarse,
    fine = sim2_tune_fine,
    coarse_best_eta1_sd = sim2_best_eta1,
    coarse_best_eta2_sd = sim2_best_eta2,
    fine_eta1_range = range(sim2_fine_eta1),
    fine_eta2_range = range(sim2_fine_eta2)
  )
  saveRDS(sim2_tune_pack, sim2_tune_file)
}

hpars_sim2_tune_stage <- if (!is.null(sim2_tune_pack$fine)) "fine" else "coarse"
sim2_tune <- if (!is.null(sim2_tune_pack$fine)) sim2_tune_pack$fine else sim2_tune_pack$coarse
hpars_sim2 <- coalesce_hpars_from_tune(sim2_tune, default_hpars_sim2, mu_override = default_hpars_sim2$mu)
hpars_realhd <- hpars_sim2
hpars_sim2_tag <- paste0("gridtune_", hpars_tag(hpars_sim2))
hpars_realhd_tag <- hpars_sim2_tag

sim2_tune_summary <- sim2_tune$results
if (nrow(sim2_tune_summary) > 0L) {
  sim2_tune_summary <- sim2_tune_summary[order(sim2_tune_summary$fdr, -sim2_tune_summary$sensitivity), , drop = FALSE]
  keep_cols <- c("eta1_sd", "eta2_sd", "mu_tilde", "eta1_tilde", "eta2_tilde", "e_eta", "f_eta", "sensitivity", "fdr", "selected_size")
  keep_cols <- keep_cols[keep_cols %in% names(sim2_tune_summary)]
  knitr::kable(
    head(sim2_tune_summary[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE], 8),
    digits = 3,
    caption = sprintf(
      "Simulation 2 eta/Moller tuning summary (%s stage, fixed PIP = 0.5, coarse eta grid seq(0.5, 5, 1), fine local refinement).",
      hpars_sim2_tune_stage
    )
  )
}
```

## Running the Sparse Samplers

In the high-dimensional regime, we invoke the **sparse** backends explicitly tailored for $p \gg n$. To optimize memory consumption during rendering, each sampler execution is isolated in a separate R chunk, with intermediate states securely serialized to disk.

```{r sim2-run-setup, cache=TRUE}
cfg_sim2 <- mcmc_scale(
  full_niter = 5000L, full_burnin = 2500L, full_thin = 2L,
  quick_niter = 5L, quick_burnin = 2L, quick_thin = 1L
)
niter_sim2 <- cfg_sim2$niter
burnin_sim2 <- cfg_sim2$burnin
thin_sim2 <- cfg_sim2$thin
sim2_n_mh_gamma <- if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 10L else 10L
pip_threshold_sim2 <- pip_threshold_fixed

# Force sparse GGM backends for high-dimensional examples
hd_sparse <- TRUE
if (!exists("hpars_sim2_tag", inherits = TRUE)) {
  hpars_sim2_tag <- paste0("gridtune_", hpars_tag(hpars_sim2))
}
sim2_hp_tag <- hpars_sim2_tag

sim2_dir <- "sim2_saved_runs"
if (!dir.exists(sim2_dir)) dir.create(sim2_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Precompute a sparse S_ggm for high-dimensional runs to avoid near-dense sweeps.
sim2_sggm_q <- if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 0.95 else 0.98
sim2_sggm_tag <- sprintf("q%02d", as.integer(round(100 * sim2_sggm_q)))
sim2_sggm_file <- file.path(
  sim2_dir, sprintf("sim2_Sggm_%s_n%d_p%d.rds", sim2_sggm_tag, nrow(X2), ncol(X2))
)
if (file.exists(sim2_sggm_file)) {
  S_ggm_sim2 <- readRDS(sim2_sggm_file)
} else {
  S_dense <- as.matrix(Matrix::crossprod(X2))
  diag_s <- diag(S_dense)
  off_abs <- abs(S_dense[upper.tri(S_dense)])
  s_thr <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(off_abs, probs = sim2_sggm_q, na.rm = TRUE))
  S_dense[abs(S_dense) < s_thr] <- 0
  diag(S_dense) <- diag_s
  S_ggm_sim2 <- Matrix::Matrix(S_dense, sparse = TRUE)
  saveRDS(S_ggm_sim2, sim2_sggm_file)
}
cat(sprintf(
  "[sim2-run-setup] sparse S_ggm ready (%s): nnz=%d, density=%.4f\n",
  sim2_sggm_tag, length(S_ggm_sim2@x), length(S_ggm_sim2@x) / (ncol(S_ggm_sim2)^2)
))

sim2_mh_file <- file.path(
  sim2_dir, sprintf("sim2_mh_sparse_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_hp_tag, niter_sim2, burnin_sim2, thin_sim2)
)
sim2_pg_file <- file.path(
  sim2_dir, sprintf("sim2_pg_sparse_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_hp_tag, niter_sim2, burnin_sim2, thin_sim2)
)
sim2_fx_file <- file.path(
  sim2_dir, sprintf("sim2_mh_fixed_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_hp_tag, niter_sim2, burnin_sim2, thin_sim2)
)
```

```{r sim2-run-mh-sparse, cache=FALSE}
if (file.exists(sim2_mh_file)) {
  mh_obj <- readRDS(sim2_mh_file)
  t_mh_sparse <- mh_obj$time
} else {
  t_mh_sparse <- system.time({
    fit_mh_sp <- bvs_mh(
      X2, y2,
      adj_type = "ggm", sparse = hd_sparse,
      S_ggm = S_ggm_sim2,
      niter = niter_sim2, burnin = burnin_sim2,
      mu = hpars_sim2$mu, n_mh_gamma = sim2_n_mh_gamma, thin = thin_sim2,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde,
      store_beta = TRUE, store_gamma = TRUE, store_Z_list = FALSE, store_Z_pip = TRUE
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit_mh_sp, time = t_mh_sparse), sim2_mh_file)
  rm(fit_mh_sp)
}
gc()
```

```{r sim2-run-pg-sparse, cache=FALSE}
if (file.exists(sim2_pg_file)) {
  pg_obj <- readRDS(sim2_pg_file)
  t_pg_sparse <- pg_obj$time
} else {
  t_pg_sparse <- system.time({
    fit_pg_sp <- bvs_pg(
      X2, y2,
      adj_type = "ggm", sparse = hd_sparse,
      S_ggm = S_ggm_sim2,
      niter = niter_sim2, burnin = burnin_sim2,
      mu = hpars_sim2$mu, n_mh_gamma = sim2_n_mh_gamma, thin = thin_sim2,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde,
      store_beta = TRUE, store_gamma = TRUE, store_Z_list = FALSE, store_Z_pip = TRUE
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit_pg_sp, time = t_pg_sparse), sim2_pg_file)
  rm(fit_pg_sp)
}
gc()
```

```{r sim2-run-mh-fixed, cache=FALSE}
if (file.exists(sim2_fx_file)) {
  fx_obj <- readRDS(sim2_fx_file)
  t_mh_fixed2 <- fx_obj$time
} else {
  t_mh_fixed2 <- system.time({
    fit_mh_fixed2 <- bvs_mh(
      X2, y2,
      adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known2,
      niter = niter_sim2, burnin = burnin_sim2,
      mu = hpars_sim2$mu, thin = thin_sim2,
      eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
      e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
      mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde
    )
  })
  saveRDS(list(fit = fit_mh_fixed2, time = t_mh_fixed2), sim2_fx_file)
  rm(fit_mh_fixed2)
}
gc()
```

```{r sim2-load-fits, cache=FALSE}
mh_obj <- readRDS(sim2_mh_file)
pg_obj <- readRDS(sim2_pg_file)
fx_obj <- readRDS(sim2_fx_file)

fit_mh_sp <- mh_obj$fit
fit_pg_sp <- pg_obj$fit
fit_mh_fixed2 <- fx_obj$fit
t_mh_sparse <- mh_obj$time
t_pg_sparse <- pg_obj$time
t_mh_fixed2 <- fx_obj$time

rm(mh_obj, pg_obj, fx_obj)
gc()
```

## Computational Profiling ($p = 1000$)

```{r sim2-timing}
timing_df2 <- data.frame(
  Method     = c("MH (sparse GGM)", "PG (sparse GGM)", "MH (fixed adj)"),
  Elapsed    = round(c(t_mh_sparse["elapsed"], t_pg_sparse["elapsed"], t_mh_fixed2["elapsed"]), 1),
  Iter_sec   = round(niter_sim2 / c(t_mh_sparse["elapsed"], t_pg_sparse["elapsed"], t_mh_fixed2["elapsed"]), 0)
)
knitr::kable(
  timing_df2,
  caption = sprintf(
    "Running time comparison in the high-dimensional setting ($n = 200, p = 1000$, %s post-burn-in iterations, burn-in %s, thin = %s).",
    format(niter_sim2, big.mark = ","),
    format(burnin_sim2, big.mark = ","),
    thin_sim2
  ),
  col.names = c("Method", "Elapsed (sec)", "Iter/sec")
)
```

## MCMC Diagnostics

### Trace Plots

```{r sim2-trace, fig.height=8, fig.width=8, fig.cap="MCMC Trace profiles for the leading four coefficients in the high-dimensional space. The true parameters are marked by dashed red lines."}
par(mfrow = c(3, 4), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1), mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0))
vars2 <- 1:4
for (method_name in c("MH (sparse GGM)", "PG (sparse GGM)", "MH (fixed)")) {
  fit_cur <- switch(method_name,
    "MH (sparse GGM)" = fit_mh_sp,
    "PG (sparse GGM)" = fit_pg_sp,
    "MH (fixed)" = fit_mh_fixed2
  )
  beta_cur <- get_beta_matrix(fit_cur)
  for (j in vars2) {
    plot(beta_cur[, j],
      type = "l", col = "steelblue",
      main = paste0(method_name, ": beta_", j),
      xlab = "Iteration", ylab = expression(beta[j]),
      cex.main = 0.8
    )
    abline(h = beta_true2[j], col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 1.5)
  }
}
```

### Model Size Tracking

```{r sim2-modelsize, fig.height=4, fig.width=8, fig.cap="Temporal trace of the effective model size (count of active predictors) traversing the MCMC chain."}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1), mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0))
plot(model_size_chain(fit_mh_sp),
  type = "l", col = "darkblue",
  main = "MH (sparse GGM)", xlab = "Iteration", ylab = "Model size"
)
abline(h = p_star2, col = "red", lty = 2)

plot(model_size_chain(fit_pg_sp),
  type = "l", col = "darkblue",
  main = "PG (sparse GGM)", xlab = "Iteration", ylab = "Model size"
)
abline(h = p_star2, col = "red", lty = 2)

plot(model_size_chain(fit_mh_fixed2),
  type = "l", col = "darkblue",
  main = "MH (fixed adj)", xlab = "Iteration", ylab = "Model size"
)
abline(h = p_star2, col = "red", lty = 2)
```

### Autocorrelation Assessment

```{r sim2-acf, fig.height=4, fig.width=8, fig.cap="ACF analysis of beta_1 within the sparse, high-dimensional estimation framework."}
par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1), mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0))
safe_acf_plot(get_beta_matrix(fit_mh_sp)[, 1], lag.max = 50, main = "MH (sparse GGM)", col = "steelblue")
safe_acf_plot(get_beta_matrix(fit_pg_sp)[, 1], lag.max = 50, main = "PG (sparse GGM)", col = "steelblue")
safe_acf_plot(get_beta_matrix(fit_mh_fixed2)[, 1], lag.max = 50, main = "MH (fixed adj)", col = "steelblue")
```

### Gelman-Rubin Convergence Diagnostics

```{r sim2-gelman, cache=TRUE, eval=TRUE}
vars_gr2 <- 1:4
seed_sim2 <- c(20261201L, 20261202L)
cfg_sim2_gr <- mcmc_scale(
  full_niter = min(200L, niter_sim2),
  full_burnin = min(80L, burnin_sim2),
  full_thin = thin_sim2,
  quick_niter = 80L,
  quick_burnin = 30L,
  quick_thin = 1L
)
niter_sim2_gr <- cfg_sim2_gr$niter
burnin_sim2_gr <- cfg_sim2_gr$burnin
thin_sim2_gr <- cfg_sim2_gr$thin
sim2_n_mh_gamma_gr <- if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 4L else min(5L, sim2_n_mh_gamma)

# Use a quick sparse approximation of S_ggm for two-chain diagnostics in p >> n.
S_ggm_gr2 <- Matrix::crossprod(X2)
S2_dense <- as.matrix(S_ggm_gr2)
diag2 <- diag(S2_dense)
thr2 <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(abs(S2_dense[upper.tri(S2_dense)]), probs = 0.90, na.rm = TRUE))
S2_dense[abs(S2_dense) < thr2] <- 0
diag(S2_dense) <- diag2
S_ggm_gr2 <- Matrix::Matrix(S2_dense, sparse = TRUE)

set.seed(seed_sim2[1])
fit_mh_sp_gr1 <- bvs_mh(
  X2, y2,
  adj_type = "ggm", sparse = TRUE, S_ggm = S_ggm_gr2,
  niter = niter_sim2_gr, burnin = burnin_sim2_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim2$mu, n_mh_gamma = sim2_n_mh_gamma_gr, thin = thin_sim2_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde,
  store_beta = TRUE, store_gamma = TRUE, store_Z_list = FALSE, store_Z_pip = TRUE
)
set.seed(seed_sim2[2])
fit_mh_sp_gr2 <- bvs_mh(
  X2, y2,
  adj_type = "ggm", sparse = TRUE, S_ggm = S_ggm_gr2,
  niter = niter_sim2_gr, burnin = burnin_sim2_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim2$mu, n_mh_gamma = sim2_n_mh_gamma_gr, thin = thin_sim2_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde,
  store_beta = TRUE, store_gamma = TRUE, store_Z_list = FALSE, store_Z_pip = TRUE
)

set.seed(seed_sim2[1])
fit_pg_sp_gr1 <- bvs_pg(
  X2, y2,
  adj_type = "ggm", sparse = TRUE, S_ggm = S_ggm_gr2,
  niter = niter_sim2_gr, burnin = burnin_sim2_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim2$mu, n_mh_gamma = sim2_n_mh_gamma_gr, thin = thin_sim2_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde,
  store_beta = TRUE, store_gamma = TRUE, store_Z_list = FALSE, store_Z_pip = TRUE
)
set.seed(seed_sim2[2])
fit_pg_sp_gr2 <- bvs_pg(
  X2, y2,
  adj_type = "ggm", sparse = TRUE, S_ggm = S_ggm_gr2,
  niter = niter_sim2_gr, burnin = burnin_sim2_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim2$mu, n_mh_gamma = sim2_n_mh_gamma_gr, thin = thin_sim2_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde,
  store_beta = TRUE, store_gamma = TRUE, store_Z_list = FALSE, store_Z_pip = TRUE
)

set.seed(seed_sim2[1])
fit_mh_fx_gr1 <- bvs_mh(
  X2, y2,
  adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known2,
  niter = niter_sim2_gr, burnin = burnin_sim2_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim2$mu, thin = thin_sim2_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde
)
set.seed(seed_sim2[2])
fit_mh_fx_gr2 <- bvs_mh(
  X2, y2,
  adj_type = "fixed", adj_fixed = R_known2,
  niter = niter_sim2_gr, burnin = burnin_sim2_gr,
  mu = hpars_sim2$mu, thin = thin_sim2_gr,
  eta1_sd = hpars_sim2$eta1_sd,
  e_eta = hpars_sim2$e_eta, f_eta = hpars_sim2$f_eta,
  mu_tilde = hpars_sim2$mu_tilde, eta1_tilde = hpars_sim2$eta1_tilde
)

gelman_sim2 <- list(
  "MH (sparse GGM)" = safe_gelman_df(list(fit_mh_sp_gr1, fit_mh_sp_gr2), vars = vars_gr2, autoburnin = FALSE),
  "PG (sparse GGM)" = safe_gelman_df(list(fit_pg_sp_gr1, fit_pg_sp_gr2), vars = vars_gr2, autoburnin = FALSE),
  "MH (fixed adj)" = safe_gelman_df(list(fit_mh_fx_gr1, fit_mh_fx_gr2), vars = vars_gr2, autoburnin = FALSE)
)

gelman_sim2_summary <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(gelman_sim2), function(mn) {
  gelman_method_summary(gelman_sim2[[mn]], mn)
}))

gelman_sim2_detail <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(gelman_sim2), function(mn) {
  df <- gelman_sim2[[mn]]
  data.frame(
    Method = mn,
    Parameter = df$Parameter,
    Point_Rhat = round(df$Point_est, 3),
    Upper_CI_Rhat = round(df$Upper_CI, 3),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

knitr::kable(
  gelman_sim2_summary,
  caption = "High-dimensional simulation Gelman-Rubin convergence summary."
)
knitr::kable(
  gelman_sim2_detail,
  caption = "Detailed high-dimensional PSRF values."
)
```

```{r sim2-gelman-trace-2chain, eval=TRUE, fig.height=10, fig.width=12, fig.cap="Simulation 2: two-chain trace plots for representative coefficients across Gelman-Rubin diagnostic runs."}
beta_idx_gr2_trace <- unique(c(1L, 3L, 10L, min(p2, p_star2 + 35L)))
par(
  mfrow = c(3, length(beta_idx_gr2_trace)),
  mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1),
  mgp = c(1.8, 0.5, 0)
)
plot_two_chain_trace_panels(fit_mh_sp_gr1, fit_mh_sp_gr2, beta_idx_gr2_trace, "MH (sparse GGM)")
plot_two_chain_trace_panels(fit_pg_sp_gr1, fit_pg_sp_gr2, beta_idx_gr2_trace, "PG (sparse GGM)")
plot_two_chain_trace_panels(fit_mh_fx_gr1, fit_mh_fx_gr2, beta_idx_gr2_trace, "MH (fixed)")
```

## Variable Selection Performance ($p \gg n$)

### Posterior Inclusion Probabilities

```{r sim2-pip, fig.height=5, fig.width=9, fig.cap="PIP profiles for the $p = 1000$ simulation. Signal variables (1-15) are designated in red, demonstrating successful isolation from the noise."}
pip_mh_sp <- compute_pip(fit_mh_sp)
pip_pg_sp <- compute_pip(fit_pg_sp)
pip_fixed2 <- compute_pip(fit_mh_fixed2)

# Show first 50 variables + any with PIP > 0.1 beyond
show_vars <- unique(c(1:50, which(pip_mh_sp > 0.1), which(pip_pg_sp > 0.1), which(pip_fixed2 > 0.1)))
show_vars <- sort(show_vars)

par(mfrow = c(1, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 2, 1))
cols2 <- ifelse(show_vars <= p_star2, "firebrick", "grey60")

barplot(pip_mh_sp[show_vars],
  col = cols2, border = NA,
  main = "MH (sparse GGM)",
  xlab = "Variable", ylab = "PIP", ylim = c(0, 1),
  names.arg = show_vars, las = 2, cex.names = 0.5
)
abline(h = pip_threshold_fixed, lty = 2)

barplot(pip_pg_sp[show_vars],
  col = cols2, border = NA,
  main = "PG (sparse GGM)",
  xlab = "Variable", ylab = "PIP", ylim = c(0, 1),
  names.arg = show_vars, las = 2, cex.names = 0.5
)
abline(h = pip_threshold_fixed, lty = 2)

barplot(pip_fixed2[show_vars],
  col = cols2, border = NA,
  main = "MH (fixed adj)",
  xlab = "Variable", ylab = "PIP", ylim = c(0, 1),
  names.arg = show_vars, las = 2, cex.names = 0.5
)
abline(h = pip_threshold_fixed, lty = 2)
```

### Selection Accuracy

```{r sim2-metrics}
metrics2 <- rbind(
  cbind(Method = "MH (sparse GGM)", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip_mh_sp, p_star = p_star2, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "PG (sparse GGM)", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip_pg_sp, p_star = p_star2, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr)),
  cbind(Method = "MH (fixed adj)", compute_metrics_fixed_pip(pip_fixed2, p_star = p_star2, pip_threshold = pip_threshold_fixed, target_fdr = sim_target_fdr))
)
knitr::kable(metrics2,
  caption = sprintf(
    "Selection accuracy benchmarks in the high-dimensional setting under fixed PIP cutoff %.2f (target FDR <= %.2f; $n = 200, p = 1000$).",
    pip_threshold_fixed, sim_target_fdr
  ),
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

## Comparative Analysis of Network Priors (High-Dimensional)

Following the protocol established in Section 5, we benchmarked the five structural network priors in the high-dimensional parameter space, relying on the `sparse = TRUE` C++ routines where applicable.

```{r sim2-adjtype-run, cache=TRUE}
sim2_adj_dir <- "sim2_adjtype_saved_runs"
if (!dir.exists(sim2_adj_dir)) dir.create(sim2_adj_dir, recursive = TRUE)

niter_adj2 <- niter_sim2
burnin_adj2 <- burnin_sim2
thin_adj2 <- thin_sim2
if (!exists("hpars_sim2_tag", inherits = TRUE)) {
  hpars_sim2_tag <- paste0("gridtune_", hpars_tag(hpars_sim2))
}
sim2_adj_hp_tag <- hpars_sim2_tag

sim2_f_fixed <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, sprintf("sim2_fixed50_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_adj_hp_tag, niter_adj2, burnin_adj2, thin_adj2))
sim2_f_ggm <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, sprintf("sim2_ggm_sparse_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_adj_hp_tag, niter_adj2, burnin_adj2, thin_adj2))
sim2_f_ggmf <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, sprintf("sim2_ggm_fixed_sparse_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_adj_hp_tag, niter_adj2, burnin_adj2, thin_adj2))
sim2_sggm_q_adj <- if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 0.90 else 0.98
sim2_sggm_tag_adj <- sprintf("q%02d", as.integer(round(100 * sim2_sggm_q_adj)))
sim2_f_sggm <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, sprintf("sim2_Sggm_%s_n%d_p%d.rds", sim2_sggm_tag_adj, nrow(X2), ncol(X2)))
sim2_f_gladj <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, "sim2_glasso_adj_ebic_cache1.rds")

# Additional speed controls for dense fixed-adjacency samplers in p >> n.
sim2_glasso_keep_ratio <- if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 0.20 else 0.05
sim2_glasso_max_degree <- if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 120L else 40L
sim2_mh_tmax_heavy <- if (isTRUE(quick_check)) 8L else 16L
sim2_glasso_tag <- sprintf(
  "spr%02d_deg%d_T%d",
  as.integer(round(100 * sim2_glasso_keep_ratio)),
  sim2_glasso_max_degree,
  sim2_mh_tmax_heavy
)
sim2_f_gladj_sparse <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, sprintf("sim2_glasso_adj_ebic_%s.rds", sim2_glasso_tag))
sim2_f_gls <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, sprintf("sim2_glasso_%s_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_glasso_tag, sim2_adj_hp_tag, niter_adj2, burnin_adj2, thin_adj2))
sim2_f_gf <- file.path(sim2_adj_dir, sprintf("sim2_glasso_fixed_%s_%s_n%d_b%d_t%d.rds", sim2_glasso_tag, sim2_adj_hp_tag, niter_adj2, burnin_adj2, thin_adj2))

cat("[sim2-adjtype] precompute S_ggm ...\n")
if (file.exists(sim2_f_sggm)) {
  t2_pre_s <- c(elapsed = 0)
  S_ggm_sim2 <- readRDS(sim2_f_sggm)
} else {
  t2_pre_s <- system.time({
    S_dense <- as.matrix(Matrix::crossprod(X2))
    diag_s <- diag(S_dense)
    off_abs <- abs(S_dense[upper.tri(S_dense)])
    s_thr <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(off_abs, probs = sim2_sggm_q_adj, na.rm = TRUE))
    S_dense[abs(S_dense) < s_thr] <- 0
    diag(S_dense) <- diag_s
    S_ggm_sim2 <- Matrix::Matrix(S_dense, sparse = TRUE)
